message:
feat(engine): implement C4 (GA readiness) and C5 (FrankenBrowser integration) cut-line gate specs

Extend the cut-line automation framework with two new milestone gates that
bring the engine from C3 (integration stability) through GA readiness (C4)
and into browser-embedding integration validation (C5).

C4 — GA Readiness & Evidence-Bound Claims:
- Adds default_c4() spec with 6 mandatory gate requirements covering
  proof-carrying CI, latency/memory budget enforcement, incident/rollback
  drill validation, catastrophic-tail adversarial resilience, observability
  sentinel health, and public claim registry evidence binding
- Introduces 8 new metadata reference key constants (C4_PROOF_CI_GATE,
  C4_LATENCY_MEMORY_BUDGET, C4_INCIDENT_ROLLBACK_DRILL, etc.) to ensure
  every gate input carries provenance links to its evidence artifacts
- Implements c4_missing_metadata_reason() validator enforcing that all
  required metadata keys are present before gate evaluation proceeds

C5 — FrankenBrowser Integration Readiness:
- Adds default_c5() spec with 6 mandatory gate requirements covering
  browser embedding boundary validation, scheduler/runtime compatibility,
  security boundary proofs, deterministic runtime replay, embedded logging
  correlation, and migration/rollback ownership surface verification
- Introduces 7 new metadata reference key constants (C5_BROWSER_EMBEDDING,
  C5_SCHEDULER_EMBEDDING_COMPAT, C5_BROWSER_SECURITY_BOUNDARY, etc.)
- Implements c5_missing_metadata_reason() validator with browser-specific
  metadata provenance enforcement
- Uses tighter staleness window (5 minutes vs C4's 10 minutes) reflecting
  the higher-frequency integration feedback loop

Integration updates:
- with_defaults() evaluator now loads C0-C5 (was C0-C3)
- Comprehensive integration tests added: make_c4_inputs() and
  make_c5_inputs() with full metadata fixture coverage
- Tests validate both passing scenarios and metadata-missing rejection paths
- Issue tracker (.beads/issues.jsonl) updated to reflect C4/C5 gate work

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
